mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital and discounts future corporate earnings more heavily, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 FEDFUNDS vintage value is 3.08, indicating ongoing tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS has increased consistently over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a weakening labor market and broader economic distress, which negatively impacts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 UNRATE vintage value is 3.7, a low but potentially turning point
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances where UNRATE has risen significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) suggests an overheating economy, which typically prompts more aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 CPIAUCSL vintage value is high (298.062)
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL are growing strongly above the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 GDPC1 revised value (22125.625) indicates strong growth
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative during periods of sustained GDPC1 growth above 2% annualized, particularly when inflation is not accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real incomes and corporate profit margins, while also forcing the central bank to maintain a restrictive monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 CPIAUCSL vintage value (298.062) indicates high inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high and persistent inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a strong and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer demand and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 PAYEMS vintage value (153308.0) indicates robust job growth
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns during periods of consistent PAYEMS growth (e.g., 3-month average > 100k jobs).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based contraction in both manufacturing and construction sectors, indicating a significant economic slowdown or recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 INDPRO and HOUST have shown recent volatility
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns when both INDPRO and HOUST are declining below their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is significantly below the prevailing inflation rate (CPIAUCSL), it indicates an accommodative monetary policy that encourages borrowing and investment, thereby supporting asset prices and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022-11-15 FEDFUNDS is 3.08 while CPIAUCSL is 298.062 (high)
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative during periods where FEDFUNDS is significantly below the inflation rate, indicating that the market does not perceive the policy as stimulative.
